company.name: Soleno Therapeutics
company.subtitle: VYKAT XR commercial launch · PWS first-in-class · Profitable year 1 · Genetic obesity expansion

market.notes: First and only approved treatment for PWS hyperphagia. 2025 revenue: $190.4M in <9 months. Q4: $91.7M (+40% QoQ). 859 active patients, 1250 start forms, 630 prescribers. ~12.5% of US addressable market (~10K patients). Pricing ~$100-150K/yr. 12% discontinuation rate (AEs). EU MAA under review (Day 120 questions answered). Guidance: ~1000 additional start forms in next 9-12 months.

EU PWS population ~2-3x US addressable. EMA review ongoing, Day 120 questions answered. If approved, launches 2027. Lower pricing in EU (~50-70% of US) but larger patient pool. Revenue modeled as Soleno's direct EU sales.

Phase 2 open-label study in patients with genetic obesities (BBS, POMC, LEPR, SH2B1, etc.). If diazoxide choline works in broader genetic obesity, addressable market expands 5-10x beyond PWS. Early stage — option value. Mechanism: KATP channel opener addresses hypothalamic dysfunction.

bear.narrative: PWS launch decelerates + EU delayed + genetic obesity fails
events: ✗ PWS patient growth stalls — 12% discontinuation rate worsens
✗ Q1 2026 seasonality deeper than expected (co-pay resets)
✗ EMA requests additional data, EU approval delayed to 2028
→ Peak US revenue $250-300M, single-indication rare disease company

base.narrative: PWS ramp continues + EU approval + genetic obesity shows signal
events: ✓ US PWS revenue reaches $350-400M run rate by end 2026
✓ EMA approves VYKAT XR — EU launch 2027
~ Genetic obesity Phase 2 shows encouraging signal
→ Peak global revenue $500-700M, expanding addressable market

bull.narrative: PWS dominant + EU blockbuster + genetic obesity franchise + M&A target
events: ✓ US PWS captures 30%+ of addressable market
✓ EU approval + rapid launch — global revenue >$800M
✓ Genetic obesity data positive — TAM expands 5-10x
✓ M&A target (only approved PWS therapy, rare disease franchise)

lastData.title: VYKAT XR $190M in <9 months — profitable year 1
points: FY2025 revenue: $190.4M (launched Q2 2025, <9 months of sales)
Net income: $20.9M — profitable in first year
859 active patients, 1,250 total start forms, 630 prescribers
12% discontinuation rate (adverse events — hypertrichosis, edema, hyperglycemia)
Guidance: ~1,000 additional start forms in next 9-12 months
Stock dropped from $90 to ~$35 on launch execution concerns

nextCatalyst.title: Q1 2026 revenue (seasonality test) + EMA decision timeline
thresholds: Q1 revenue vs Q4 $91.7M — co-pay resets create seasonal dip
New patient start forms pace — are they hitting ~250/quarter?
Genetic obesity Phase 2 interim data updates

risks: Q1 2026 seasonal dip (co-pay resets, insurance plan transitions) could spook market
12% AE discontinuation rate — if it worsens, limits addressable market
COGS increasing as zero-cost pre-approval inventory depletes
PWS is small market (~10K US patients) — ceiling on US revenue
EU approval not guaranteed — EMA may have different bar than FDA
Genetic obesity expansion is Phase 2 — high scientific risk
Stock down 60% from highs — market pricing in slower ramp than expected
CFO retirement creates management transition concern
Single-product company until genetic obesity data reads out
